title: A LITLE briefly: To address this question, using RBV, we developed a multidimensional model by conceptualizing how DT of…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: A LITLE briefly: To address this question, using RBV, we developed a multidimensional model by conceptualizing how DT of hospitals can be enabled by physical IT infrastructure, IT capability dimensions, and strategic alignment and how they are interrelated.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: The Resource-Based View (RBV) theory is used to understand the capabilities of organizations and how they can use their resources to gain competitive advantage. In this study, the researchers developed a multidimensional model to explore the relationship between the digital transformation (DT) of hospitals and various aspects of their IT infrastructure, capabilities, and strategic alignment. The model suggests that by leveraging physical IT infrastructure, implementing effective IT capabilities, and aligning their strategies with the needs of their stakeholders, hospitals can effectively enable their DT processes. By understanding the interrelationships between these dimensions, hospital administrators can develop effective strategies to drive their digital transformation efforts and build a sustainable competitive advantage.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Please summarize: To address this question, using RBV, we developed a multidimensional model by conceptualizing how DT of hospitals can be enabled by physical IT infrastructure, IT capability dimensions, and strategic alignment and how they are interrelated.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: The study developed a multidimensional model using RBV to explore the relationship between digital transformation in hospitals and physical IT infrastructure, IT capabilities and strategic alignment. The model suggests that hospitals can enable their DT processes by leveraging these dimensions and understanding their interrelationships. By doing so, hospitals can develop effective strategies for sustainable competitive advantage.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: The study developed an empirical model using RBV to explore the role of physical IT infrastructure in digital transformation in hospitals through IT capabilities dimensions and strategic alignment.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Revised: This study developed an empirical model using RBV to explore the role of physical IT infrastructure in enabling digital transformation in hospitals. The model includes IT capabilities dimensions and strategic alignment as important factors that impact the success of digital transformation. Understanding these dimensions and how they interrelate can help hospitals develop effective strategies for sustainable competitive advantage.
